summary: Strict kind enforcement (Stage 4) silently no-ops for the two realistic `as image strict` forms — a path literal ("real.gif") and a read-lifted image.@this — because TryInstantiateValidator only builds a probe from raw byte[], and image.ValidateKind only reads byte[]. The C# Cut2 tests pass only because they feed raw bytes; the three PLang .test.goal files that claim to cover the mismatch cases pass while asserting nothing (a PNG is accepted as a strict GIF, build and runtime). Root cause is the unresolved Stage-9 (lazy path-handles, no I/O at set) vs Stage-4 (validate content) tension; no deferred check exists on BytesAsync either. F2 (medium): kind is serialized twice — inside the structured `type` entity and as the flat Data.Kind sibling key — OBP smell #6, branch-introduced. F3-F5 minor: type.Scheme null-deref, text-kind gate at call site, dead CanonicaliseKind fast-path. Build clean, C# 3818/3818, PLang 263/263 — green but the strict-image tests are false-confidence.
